events.text: Patient is a 38-year-old female of Asian race with an ECOG performance status of 0
At age 38, patient presented with a painless enlarging right cervical lymph node noted for six weeks without B symptoms
At age 38, excisional biopsy of the right cervical lymph node showed diffuse large B-cell lymphoma, ICD-O site C77.9, histology code 9680/3

At age 38, pathology report demonstrated centroblastic morphology with Ki-67 of 75 percent, immunohistochemistry positive for CD20, CD19, CD10, BCL6, negative for MUM1, BCL2 weakly positive, consistent with germinal center B-cell (GCB) subtype by Hans algorithm
At age 38, flow cytometry on lymph node specimen revealed monoclonal B-cell population positive for CD20, CD19, CD10, BCL6, negative for CD5, CD23, MUM1
At age 38, FISH analysis showed no MYC rearrangement, no BCL2 rearrangement, no BCL6 rearrangement; non-double-hit DLBCL
At age 38, laboratory workup showed LDH at upper limit of normal at 245 U/L, CBC with WBC 7.1, hemoglobin 13.8 g/dL, platelets 278, CMP entirely within normal limits

At age 38, staging PET/CT revealed FDG-avid right cervical and right supraclavicular lymphadenopathy with largest node measuring 5.8 cm (bulky), SUVmax 18.3, no other sites of disease above or below the diaphragm
At age 38, bone marrow biopsy was negative for lymphoma involvement with normal trilineage hematopoiesis

At age 38, patient was staged as Ann Arbor Stage II bulky (contiguous right cervical and supraclavicular nodes), no B symptoms, IPI score 2 (low-intermediate risk) based on bulky disease and elevated LDH borderline
At age 38, patient initiated cycle 1 of R-CHOP at full standard doses: rituximab 375 mg/m2, cyclophosphamide 750 mg/m2, doxorubicin 50 mg/m2, vincristine 1.4 mg/m2, prednisone 100 mg daily for 5 days
At age 38, after cycle 1 of R-CHOP, patient experienced grade 2 nausea and grade 1 alopecia, managed with ondansetron and dexamethasone
At age 38, patient received cycle 2 of R-CHOP without complications, pegfilgrastim administered on day 2
At age 38, patient received cycle 3 of R-CHOP on schedule without dose modifications
At age 38, interim PET/CT after 3 cycles showed complete resolution of cervical and supraclavicular lymphadenopathy, Deauville score 2, indicating complete metabolic response
At age 38, patient received cycle 4 of R-CHOP without complications
At age 38, patient received cycle 5 of R-CHOP, continued to tolerate well
At age 38, patient completed cycle 6 of R-CHOP without significant toxicities
At age 38, end-of-treatment PET/CT confirmed sustained complete metabolic response, Deauville score 1, no residual FDG-avid disease
At age 38, given initial bulky Stage II disease, patient received consolidative involved-site radiation therapy to the right cervical and supraclavicular region, 30 Gy in 15 fractions
At age 38, patient completed consolidative radiation without significant acute toxicity and was transitioned to surveillance
At age 39, surveillance CT at 6 months post-treatment showed no evidence of recurrent disease
At age 40, patient remains in complete remission at 2-year follow-up with normal LDH and unremarkable physical examination

scenario_blurb: Diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (DLBCL), germinal center and activated B-cell subtypes. Ann Arbor stage II-IV. Treated with R-CHOP. Include IPI scoring, PET-CT response assessment, some with relapse requiring R-ICE salvage and autologous stem cell transplant.
